promotion_tool: governed dev→release promotion (copy allow-listed changed files + ZIP)
store_upload: NONE — no Chrome Web Store upload performed by this promotion
github_release: NONE — no GitHub Release created by this promotion
wildcard_host_permissions: NONE — AG-363 removed https://*/* from manifest.chrome.json and manifest.firefox.json (unchanged by this promotion)
note: Governed dev→release promotion of AG-PROMPT-374 (Office extraction success-semantics fix). Only three allow-listed files changed since the AG-372 promotion lineage (source commit a50cb00): src/extraction/xlsxExtractor.ts, src/extraction/pptxSelectiveExtractor.ts, and src/content/metadataExtractor.ts (confirmed via git diff against a50cb00, restricted to the full AG-269 allow-list, and independently via a full recursive src/ tree diff between dev and release before this promotion — no other allow-listed file touched; src/content/index.ts, the AG-369 Copilot fix carrier, is unchanged since AG-372 and already correctly present). AG-374 root cause: XLSX's and PPTX's inner extractors could report success:true with a completely empty bodyText, merely because a supporting ZIP entry existed (xl/sharedStrings.xml for XLSX, docProps/core.xml for PPTX), regardless of whether any actual text was extracted. Because extractXlsxMetadataSelective/extractPptxMetadataSelective always return a truthy metadata:{raw:{}}, and index.ts's scannedSources.add('metadata') fires whenever success&&metadata is true, this vacuous success silently suppressed even the low-severity info.unscanned_file_type fallback signal — a genuinely-failed extraction produced total silence, not just a degraded warning. Fix: both inner extractors (xlsxExtractor.ts, pptxSelectiveExtractor.ts) now require bodyText.length > 0 strictly, matching docxSelectiveExtractor.ts's pre-existing model (DOCX unchanged, no related issue found). XLSX's outer wrapper (metadataExtractor.ts) gained the same legitimate metadata-fields fallback DOCX/PPTX's outer wrappers already had (result.success || Object.keys(metadata).length > 1); PPTX's outer wrapper was already correct and remains unchanged. This is scan-status correctness only — no detection threshold, RiskSignal structure, deduplication, or chunking logic changed. AG-369/370/371 (previously promoted in AG-372) remain included unchanged: handleDrop's event.stopImmediatePropagation() Copilot drag/drop Cancel fix, and the shared toRealmSafeBytes() same-realm deep-copy helper for DOCX/XLSX/PPTX Firefox Xray-wrapper extraction parity. Legacy .doc/.xls/.ppt remain explicitly unsupported (unchanged). No manifest, permission, or host-scope change (permissions: ['storage'] only; host_permissions limited to the 6 listed patterns; no activeTab; no wildcard/https://*/*; no <all_urls>; connect-src 'none'). No telemetry, network diagnostics, or centralized logging added; no raw document content, file names, or personal data logged or persisted; production bundle strips all console.log/console.debug via terser pure_funcs (verified — zero new diagnostic calls exist in either changed extractor, structurally confirmed before promotion). Dev-side verification (this promotion): tsc 0 errors; CORE 52/52 (incl. test-copilot-drop-coverage, test-firefox-docx-parity 13/13, test-office-zip-realm-copy 23/23, test-office-success-semantics 21/21 — new AG-374 suite); e2e 6/6 (drag-drop incl. both AG-369 Copilot-pattern Cancel/Continue tests, docx-upload); verify-manifest 28/28; Chrome build 34/34 + 20/20 consumer-copy; Firefox build 25/25. Firefox live-runtime e2e (interception-smoke.firefox.spec.ts) remains environment-blocked — 3 skipped, re-verified unchanged in this run (pre-existing AG-213 geckodriver 0.37.0 / Firefox 151.0.2 extension-injection incompatibility, not a new failure and not caused by this fix). The AG-374 fix is therefore structurally proven (source-level correctness via Node/tsx extraction+detection tests including empty-body/metadata-fallback isolation tests) but the underlying AG-370/371 Firefox DOCX/XLSX/PPTX fixes remain not yet operator-confirmed on a real Firefox browser runtime — recommended before/alongside upload, per the pending operator smoke-test checklist. Release gate results and final ZIP artifact hygiene recorded after build (see report). Permissions: ['storage'] only; no wildcard host access; connect-src 'none'. No store upload; no GitHub Release. No website repo modified.
